because.text: Kimi K3 (OpenRouter) reviewed the spec adversarially; each finding verified against both impls before disposition. ONE confirmed live cross-impl split: Go base verify swallowed an unexecutable ski@v1 check (continue) while Python WARNed -> 4-vs-3 warnings; Go now WARNs to match and escalates to ERR under settlement when the reason is settlement-active. Co-signature griefing: an invalid signature is now a WARN and is excluded, not fatal; a record errs only if no valid signature by body.actor.id remains, so a lone junk co-sig can no longer invalidate a good record (this alters a verification OUTCOME and is the one change flagged for ratification -- this warrant is that ratification). ski@v1 re-execution budget (default 100M ATP, identical in both, env-overridable): an over-budget check is reported unverified, never evaluated -- closes the uint32-atp DoS. Duplicate member names rejected as invalid I-JSON in both (object_pairs_hook / token-scanner), not last-wins. SPEC: recursive unknown-field rule + code-point lengths (2), unknown-runtime reject (3), Book I pin + re-exec budget (3.1), normative escaping + dup-name reject (4), Ed25519 acceptance S<L + co-sig exclusion (5), individual sig verification + rotation-subject-is-a-blob fix + step (7) ski re-execution severity model (6). Deferred: cofactor/small-order Ed25519 differential vector; a §8 escaping vector (differential.py already covers it). Verified locally on the MacBook; own store green.

subject.note: Kimi K3 spec review: fixed a confirmed PY/GO ski@v1-unverified split, co-signature griefing, ski re-exec budget, dup-key I-JSON; SPEC 2-6 hardened. Both impls byte-identical, suite green.
